summary: Conjecture 11 from arXiv:2405.10854 proposes that every graph triangulating some surface has log-concave genus distribution. It was introduced by Mohar as a plausible strengthening of unimodality (Conjecture 10) and a positive replacement for the disproved Gross–Robbins–Tucker LCGD Conjecture. The paper (originally submitted May 2024, revised December 2025) is very recent, and no follow-up work resolving or partially addressing this specific conjecture was found in the indexed literature.
search_queries: log-concave genus distribution triangulates surface graph Mohar 2405.10854
genus distribution log-concavity triangulation graph surface 2025 2026
notes: No follow-up found in web searches or by checking citing papers. The conjecture is very recent (2024/2025) and highly specific — restricted to graphs that triangulate some surface — which distinguishes it from the general LCGD Conjecture disproved in the same paper. The paper arXiv:2212.09971 (Annals of Combinatorics 2025) on non-log-concave cubic genus polynomials does not cite 2405.10854 and predates it.
